summary: Published and dogfooded an approval-gated incident command decision over the runx agency spine with an immutable registry package, a green two-case harness, and a verified post-publish receipt.

commands.dogfood: runx skill liomilet4-png/incident-commander@sha-af08d8fac7af advance --registry https://api.runx.ai --skip-operator-context -i case_id=incident-112-dogfood-20260715-134835 -i driver_id=incident-commander-postapproval -i incident_objective=send --input-json case_state=<recorded-json> --input-json roster=<recorded-json> --input-json approval=<recorded-json> --json

approval.reason: Approved the digest-bound checkout status update for the declared stakeholder audience.

observations.summary: Publishing ran with runx-cli 0.6.15; clean install, direct registry dogfood, and receipt verification ran with runx-cli 0.7.2. Both satisfy the required minimum of 0.6.14.

Publisher liomilet4-png published manifest version 0.1.1 of the exact incident-commander package as immutable registry version sha-af08d8fac7af with digest sha256:049b1f45b5fee3af58c91a7e0357cb8a5329a56356d2140795c5a2917804a4c2.
The live registry page, public PR 330, public source branch, and raw X.yaml and SKILL.md URLs all identify the same incident-commander package.

The local inline harness passed with zero assertion errors and the hosted registry publish passed; case incident-send-awaits-then-approves sealed and case incident-assign-missing-owner stopped with needs_agent.
For a declared SEV-2 checkout-api incident, the pre-approval turn returned awaiting_approval with executable=false in human:incident-reviewer.

The turn named send-as runner plan for principal incident:comms:morgan, audience stakeholders:checkout-api with classification incident-stakeholders, and content_digest sha256:4e44f31f628799267f0957c554b8c47d90d2eabf41e5a84248941d28c45955ae.
After approval principal incident:comms:morgan matched the comms_lead roster entry, the post-approval turn returned advanced with executable=true; the skill still performed no provider delivery.
For an assign objective with no named owner, ops-desk remained needs_agent and no named run was emitted rather than inventing a responder.
The direct post-publish registry dogfood run sealed receipt sha256:698755f06556247d4e7226a42e2906e903df0d848d43fd5a1d76742d63f90025; runx verify returned exit code 0.
A clean runx add of liomilet4-png/incident-commander@sha-af08d8fac7af completed successfully before the direct registry dogfood run.
runx registry read resolved owner, exact package name, immutable version, digest, profile digest, runner advance, and public install/run commands.
The rehearsal performed no provider send, purchase, payment, account mutation, or other external business write.
